Nanah (Cassia auriculata) is a plant species in the family Fabaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia and Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Cassia auriculata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Cassia auriculata is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive disorders.
taxonomy: Nania ciliata is classified in the plant kingdom, specifically within the family Gesneriaceae. It belongs to the genus Nania, which is a group of flowering plants native to Africa.
